summary: Matched the ftCommon FlyReflect object by reshaping ftCo_FlyReflect.c codegen, matching its .sdata2 constants, and switching the file from NonMatching to Matching. The work used stack-padding locals/PAD_STACK, forced no-inline plus a separate inline clone for ftCo_800C1718, a PPC-style fake_sqrtf using __frsqrte, and explicit branch temporaries in collision paths. Automated decomp.dev output reported 4 new GALE01 matches in this unit, including .sdata2, ftCo_800C17CC, ftCo_800C18A8, and ftCo_FlyReflect_Coll.

key_files.role: Main matched source; adjusted includes, stack pads, inlining/no-inlining, fake_sqrtf, and collision control flow.
Renamed five .sdata2 constants from ftCo_804D8C40-style address names to compiler local @221/@259/@260/@261/@262 labels.

what_changed: Marked melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_FlyReflect.c as Matching in configure.py.
Reworked ftCo_FlyReflect.c includes: added placeholder.h and ftCommon/inlines.h while removing several direct platform/fighter/math includes shown in the diff.
Changed stack padding in ftCo_800C15F4 and ftCo_800C1718 from initialized byte arrays to scalar u32 pad locals; ftCo_800C17CC also gained PAD_STACK(0x10).
Wrapped ftCo_800C1718 in #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop and changed its fighter access from GET_FIGHTER(gobj) to gobj->user_data.
Added ftCo_800C1718_inline, an inline duplicate of the ceiling-reflect logic, and used it in ftCo_FlyReflect_Coll for one RETURN_IF site.
Expanded ftCo_800C17CC from a simple ftCo_800C15F4/ftCo_800C1718 wrapper into explicit wall/ceiling reflect logic with local ret and exact return shape.
Added inline fake_sqrtf using __frsqrte, three Newton refinement steps, pad locals, and volatile float y; ftCo_800C18A8 now uses fake_sqrtf instead of sqrtf for the velocity magnitude.
Adjusted ftCo_800C18A8 local declaration/assignment shape around Fighter* fp and removed an old u8 stack pad array.
Reworked ftCo_FlyReflect_Coll to introduce s32 tmp and spell out the ftCo_800C15F4 then ftCo_800C1718 decision instead of RETURN_IF(ftCo_800C17CC(gobj)).
Updated GALE01 .sdata2 symbols for addresses 0x804D8C40 through 0x804D8C58 to @221, @259, @260, @261, and @262.
smart_moves: Used both a forced-out-of-line ftCo_800C1718 and a separate ftCo_800C1718_inline clone, acknowledging that different call sites needed different inlining behavior to match.

Replaced standard sqrtf with a hand-written fake_sqrtf that mirrors the PPC reciprocal-square-root sequence, including __frsqrte refinement and volatile storage, instead of fighting libm/codegen differences.
Matched branch structure by introducing explicit ret/tmp locals and return 1/0 forms rather than keeping cleaner RETURN_IF abstraction everywhere.
Addressed the author's stated stack-pad concern without relying on initialized byte arrays that could create unwanted rodata, using u32 pad locals and PAD_STACK(0x10) where needed.
Completed the object match by updating .sdata2 symbol labels as well as source code, so data and code percentages both reached 100% for reported items.
decomp_lessons: For small near-matching functions, source-level cleanliness may need to yield to exact local order, temporary variables, and branch spelling.
A function can need a noinline standalone body while the same logic is manually duplicated or wrapped in an inline helper at another call site.
Stack padding should be chosen carefully; initialized byte arrays can affect rodata, while scalar pads or PAD_STACK may be safer matching tools.
GET_FIGHTER(gobj) versus direct gobj->user_data can matter for codegen and should be tried when register/load order is off.
Matching math calls may require reproducing the compiler's inline PPC sequence instead of calling sqrtf directly.
Do not ignore .sdata2 naming and locality: compiler-style @NNN labels may be necessary for object/data matching even when the constants were previously address-named.
naming_conventions: Unknown or not-yet-semantically-named common functions remained address-style, e.g. ftCo_800C15F4, ftCo_800C1718, ftCo_800C17CC, and ftCo_800C18A8.
The inline clone was named ftCo_800C1718_inline, explicitly tying it to the out-of-line ftCo_800C1718 while documenting its matching purpose.
The sqrt helper was named fake_sqrtf, making it clear this is a codegen/matching stand-in for sqrtf rather than a newly identified game symbol.
Motion and surface enums use existing descriptive ftCo names such as ftCo_MS_FlyReflectWall, ftCo_MS_FlyReflectCeil, ftCo_Surface_LeftWall, and ftCo_Surface_Ceiling.
Compiler-generated .sdata2 labels were represented as @221, @259, @260, @261, and @262 instead of ftCo_804D8C40-style address symbols.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: #pragma dont_inline on around ftCo_800C1718, scoped with #pragma push/pop.
Created a separate inline function for ftCo_800C1718 logic to satisfy call sites that wanted inlined ceiling-reflect behavior.
Used PAD_STACK(0x10), u32 pad locals, and explicit local declarations to tune stack frame/layout.
Introduced explicit s32 ret/tmp variables and if (ret != false) / if (tmp != 0) checks to shape branches and returns.
Used direct gobj->user_data in ftCo_800C1718 where GET_FIGHTER(gobj) did not match the desired codegen.
Implemented fake_sqrtf with __frsqrte and three Newton iterations, plus volatile float y, to match PPC sqrt-like code generation.
Renamed .sdata2 symbols to @NNN local labels to match emitted data labels.
Separated Fighter* declaration from assignment in ftCo_800C18A8, a common MWCC scheduling/register matching nudge.
review_feedback: No human review comments or formal reviews were present in the slice.
Author noted this was their first match PR and specifically asked about how to do the stack pad at the beginning of a function without creating rodata.
Automated decomp.dev report for GALE01 showed matched code 59.14% (+0.03%, +1268 bytes), linked code 33.30% (+0.05%, +1860 bytes), matched data +32 bytes, and linked data +32 bytes.
Automated report listed 4 new matches: ftCo_FlyReflect .sdata2 to 100%, ftCo_800C17CC to 100%, ftCo_800C18A8 to 100%, and ftCo_FlyReflect_Coll to 100%.
